_comment: Pinned reference values for NEAR AI Cloud TEE backends (mainnet). Captured 2026-05-05 from https://cloud-api.near.ai/v1/attestation/report. The Base on-chain DstackKms/DstackApp registries are the authoritative source for app_id/compose_hash/os_image_hash/kms_pubkey; until hermes ships an RPC reader (Block B), we pin known-good values here. inner_yaml pins the inner compose closure (Block C) — without it the operator can swap the inner YAML in nearai/cvm-compose-files (which is fetched at runtime by compose-manager and NOT in the outer compose_hash measurement) and exfiltrate prompts on the same signing key. Refresh on operator rotation. Source captures: case-studies/near-ai-private-inference/sources/.

zai-org/GLM-5.1-FP8._note: Outer compose_hash rotated within ~30min on 2026-05-05 from 0x700adbf5… → 0x242a6272… on the same signing_address — operator can rotate compose without rotating signing keys. Both kept until clarified.

deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1._status: model substitution observed 2026-05-05: requesting deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1 returns model_name=Qwen/Qwen3.5-122B-A10B (file=Qwen3.5-122B.yaml). Hermes' model_name check (added in this PR) fails closed on this. inner_yaml left empty until DeepSeek-V3.1 is restored to its own backend.

openai/gpt-oss-120b._note: Inner YAML is small-models.yaml — a multi-model compose hosting gpt-oss-120b alongside qwen3-30b/qwen3-vl/qwen3-embeddings/qwen3-reranker/whisper3-large/flux. Each model gets its own vllm-proxy-* service in one CVM. MODEL_NAME env per service determines KMS-derived signing key.
